error_message: E2E getJwt() sends {user_id} body and reads data.access_token — both wrong for Java iam-service API
root_cause: Python user-mgmt POST /auth/token expected {user_id: string} and returned {access_token}. Java iam-service expects {username, password} and returns {accessToken}.
fix: Changed body to {username: userId, password: 'Meridian@2024'} and read data.accessToken

error_message: 03-jwt-identity.spec.ts: health, JWKS, token, membership tests all fail after iam-service migration
root_cause: Tests were written for the Python user-mgmt service. Java iam-service uses GET /actuator/health, /oauth2/jwks (no 'use'/'alg'/'kid' fields), {username,password} token request, and PATCH /users/{id}/book for membership changes.
fix: Rewrote all 4 test categories for the Java iam-service API shape

error_message: 08-domain-authz.spec.ts: rm_jane and rm_diaz JWT fetch uses {user_id} and body.access_token
root_cause: Direct /auth/token calls at lines 231-265 still used old Python API contract
fix: Changed to {username, password} body and body.accessToken field

error_message: AuthController JWT missing segments, clearance, book, admin_domains, audience claims
root_cause: JWT issued by iam-service lacked claims needed by Cerbos policy evaluation and gateway identity resolution
fix: Added ObjectMapper injection, extractClearance/extractSegments helpers, book/adminDomains from UserResponse, audience(['meridian-gateway']) to JWT builder

error_message: PATCH /users/{id}/book returns 500: column 'metadata' is of type jsonb but expression is of type character varying
root_cause: Hibernate maps String field to character varying by default. The column is jsonb. Inserting null causes a type mismatch.

error_message: Gateway returns 401 for valid iam-service JWT: invalid iss and missing aud claim
root_cause: Gateway VALID_ISSUERS was hardcoded to ['meridian-user-mgmt', 'http://user-mgmt:8084']. iam-service JWT has iss=http://iam-service:8084 and no aud claim.
fix: Made required-issuers configurable via @Value (includes all iam-service URL variants). Added audience(['meridian-gateway']) to AuthController JWT builder.

error_message: JwksClient fails with 'Invalid JSON object': Java HttpClient does not follow redirects by default
root_cause: Java HttpClient.Redirect.NEVER is the default. /.well-known/jwks.json returns 301 to /oauth2/jwks. HttpClient got the redirect response (HTML/empty body), JWKSet.parse() failed.
fix: Added .followRedirects(HttpClient.Redirect.NORMAL) to HttpClient builder. Also changed MERIDIAN_AUTH_JWKS_URL in docker-compose to point directly to /oauth2/jwks.

error_message: Cerbos denies rm_jane all wealth agents: clearance-pii threshold was 3, rm_jane has clearance 2
root_cause: Cerbos policy required clearance >= 3 for confidential-pii. All wealth agents are data_classification=confidential-pii. rm_jane (and all standard RMs) have clearance 2.
fix: Changed confidential-pii threshold from >= 3 to >= 2. Portfolio PII is a core RM function; restricted trading data (clearance 3) is the right cutoff for 'restricted'.

error_message: E2E users rm_diaz and da_wpb do not exist in iam-service database
root_cause: Tests reference rm_diaz (dual-segment RM) and da_wpb (domain_admin) but only rm_jane was seeded in V2__seed_demo_data.sql
fix: Created new Flyway migration V3__seed_e2e_users.sql seeding both users with correct roles and attributes (clearance, segments, admin_domains)

error_message: 04-entitlements.spec.ts:12 and :51 timeout — 'Target page, context or browser has been closed' at helpers.ts:109
root_cause: Playwright test timeout was 90_000ms. LibreChat browser UI tests require ~15-20s login + ~60-80s LLM synthesis = 75-100s, exceeding the timeout. When timeout fires, Playwright closes the page context and page.waitForTimeout() throws 'page closed'.
fix: Increased timeout from 90_000 to 180_000ms in playwright.config.ts. Both UI tests now complete in ~90-96s and pass within 180s.

error_message: DomainManifestStore init fails: named capturing group is missing trailing } — Failed to parse domain manifest wealth-management.json
root_cause: resolveEnvVars() called Matcher.appendReplacement() with unquoted m.group(0) as fallback when env var was not set. m.group(0) contains ${WEALTH_COVERAGE_URL} which Matcher.appendReplacement interprets as a named back-reference, throwing IllegalArgumentException.
fix: Changed fallback to use Matcher.quoteReplacement(m.group(0)) so ${ is treated as literal text.

error_message: Insurance query naming POL-77001 wrongly clarifies 'which policy?' once a second domain is onboarded; gateway checks relationship_id instead of policy_id
root_cause: handleFetchData derived coverageEntity from coverageEntityType() which uses manifestStore.requiredContextKeys()/entityTypes() — the cross-domain UNION across ALL sub-domains — and picked the first key (relationship_id from wealth). The coverage entity was never scoped to the routed sub-domain.
fix: Added DomainManifestStore.entityTypesFor(subDomainId) (single sub-domain's entity_types) and scoped overloads coverageEntityType(EffectiveManifest)/secondaryEntityType(EffectiveManifest). In handleFetchData, once the resource-scoped agent's EffectiveManifest em is known, re-scope coverageEntity/secondaryEntity/carriedCoverageId from em.requiredContext()+em.subDomainId() before the coverage resolve/check/bind. Follow-up/session paths now use carriedCoverageEntityType(session) which finds whichever required entity the session actually carries (domain-agnostic).

error_message: LibreChat OIDC SSO: 'Callback authentication failed — server responded with an error in the response body'; login failed for rm_jane
root_cause: TWO bugs. (1) Client meridian-librechat registered only for CLIENT_SECRET_BASIC, but LibreChat's openid-client sends credentials via client_secret_post -> Spring AS rejected with 'Client authentication failed: authentication_method' -> invalid_client. (2) id_token/userinfo carried only `sub` because the JWT customizer enriched ACCESS_TOKEN only; LibreChat needs email/name to provision the user.
fix: (1) Added ClientAuthenticationMethod.CLIENT_SECRET_POST to the librechat RegisteredClient (alongside BASIC). (2) Extended JwtClaimsCustomizer to handle ID_TOKEN and added OidcClaimEnricher.enrichIdToken() returning email, email_verified, preferred_username, name (from principal + display_name attr). Verified full flow via curl: client_secret_post exchange -> HTTP 200, id_token+userinfo carry email/name, LibreChat callback -> 'login success', openid user created in Mongo.

OpenAI-compatible clie | Also: > Create a detailed technical architecture diagram; > Manager" → a box "LibreChat (chat UI, OpenAI-com

error_message: Wrong reference: List should be ArrayList

Gateway /v1/chat/completions accepts unauthenticated requests: no-identity resolves to 'anonymous' (HTTP 200), and an unknown/forged X-User-Id is processed. Auth is permitAll; only entitlement gates data.
root_cause: X-User-Id trusted-hop fallback exists because LibreChat can't forward the SSO JWT. permitAll + 'anonymous' default means requests without credentials still run (burn LLM calls); X-User-Id is forgeable. Safety currently rests on network isolation only.
fix: DEFERRED to the planned LibreChat rewrite (new UI will forward the JWT). Then: require valid JWT / reject anonymous+unknown (401); drop Redis PrincipalStore + seed. The proper JWT path already works (Axiom token -> Bearer -> gateway resolves principal from claims, no Redis). Interim safeguard: keep gateway network-isolated (not publicly exposed).
Changed to "Access denied for this client relationship."

error_message: QA §2 browser run: LibreChat hero answer rendered but #send-button stayed disabled beyond 180s, blocking follow-up. Pre-rebuild run also showed settlement/cash unavailable due MCP timeouts.
root_cause: Not fixed in this session. Evidence: Playwright screenshot shows rendered answer with disabled send button; gateway logs show full fan-out succeeded post-rebuild and AnswerSynthesizer call started, but LibreChat did not consider the stream complete within the QA timeout. Pre-rebuild servicing MCP emitted Starlette AssertionError and gateway timed out both MCP tools.
fix: Pending investigation. Check SSE completion/DONE delivery to LibreChat and whether LibreChat is waiting on a generation/title/summarize job despite titleConvo=false. Separately inspect servicing MCP ASGI middleware errors seen before rebuild.

error_message: Inline Python SSE parser commands failed with SyntaxError: unexpected character after line continuation character.
root_cause: Used single-quoted python -c strings containing literal \n sequences instead of shell $'...' quoting, so Python received backslash-n text.
fix: Reran the checks with shell $'...' quoting so Python received real newlines.

root_cause: No early return/throw for edge case: messages == null || messages.isEmpty(
fix: Added guard clause: if (messages == null || messages.isEmpty()

root_cause: Code path had no error handling — exceptions would propagate uncaught

error_message: Multi-turn client pivot: naming a new client (Okafor) after another (Whitman) answered against the prior client — no re-resolution, no entitlement re-check (Codex QA #2/#3)
root_cause: (1) combined intent+extraction echoed the prior turn's resolved id from history as the entity reference; (2) coverage pipeline precedence inverted — session-carried id won over a client named this turn (resolve was only in the else-branch).
fix: (1) scope extraction to the LATEST user message + deterministic guard dropping extracted values matching the manifest id_pattern absent from that message; (2) flip precedence — a client named THIS turn resolves + re-authorizes; carried id reused only when none named.

error_message: mvn -q -Dtest=JwtClaimsCustomizerTest test failed: Mockito could not initialize inline Byte Buddy mock maker / could not self-attach to current VM
root_cause: The local JDK 23 runtime does not allow Mockito inline mock maker self-attachment in this sandboxed test run.
fix: Replaced Mockito usage with a tiny CapturingEnricher test fake that overrides OidcClaimEnricher methods directly.

error_message: mvn -q test failed: reference to assertThat is ambiguous for claims.getClaim("clearance")
root_cause: JwtClaimsSet#getClaim is generic, and AssertJ could not infer whether the raw numeric claim should use Predicate/IntPredicate overloads.
fix: Cast the claim to Integer before asserting equality.

error_message: python3 -m pytest tests/schema failed during pytest_configure with PermissionError: [Errno 1] Operation not permitted while pytest_rerunfailures tried to bind 127.0.0.1
root_cause: The sandbox blocks localhost socket binding during pytest plugin setup; the test suite itself had not collected yet.
fix: Reran the same targeted pytest command with approved unsandboxed execution; tests then collected and ran normally.

error_message: test_manifest_cross_references_are_consistent failed: meridian.servicing.nav had sub_domain cash-management but was listed under corporate-actions
root_cause: The agent manifest sub_domain field drifted from the sub-domain manifest membership and existing docs.
fix: Changed meridian.servicing.nav sub_domain to corporate-actions so agent, sub-domain manifest, and docs agree.

error_message: openwolf designqc --routes /login / failed because the auto-started dev server command `npm run dev` exited with code 127
root_cause: DesignQC's spawned dev-server environment could not execute npm even though npm and node are available on the interactive shell PATH.
fix: Recorded the tooling failure; UI changes continued with npm build verification planned.

error_message: npm run build failed with `sh: tsc: command not found`
root_cause: The admin-ui workspace had no installed node_modules, so the build script could not find the local TypeScript compiler.
fix: Ran npm ci from admin-ui using the existing package-lock, then reran npm run build successfully.

error_message: Vite dev server failed with `listen EPERM: operation not permitted 127.0.0.1:5174`; sandboxed OpenWolf DesignQC browser launch also failed with `Failed to launch the browser process`
root_cause: The managed sandbox blocked local port listening and browser process launch for screenshot QA.
fix: Reran npm run dev with approved escalation, then reran OpenWolf DesignQC with approved browser escalation and captured screenshots successfully.

error_message: npm run build failed in admin-ui: sh: tsc: command not found
root_cause: admin-ui node_modules were absent in this worktree, so the local TypeScript binary from devDependencies was not installed.
fix: Ran npm ci in admin-ui, then reran npm run build successfully.

error_message: Workbench slice review: not mergeable as a giant page/root API file; native EventSource cannot send Authorization headers; non-streaming chat should be labeled MVP.
root_cause: Initial slice concentrated page state, API types, formatting, trace parsing, and panel components in admin-ui/src/pages/Workbench.tsx and used native EventSource.
fix: Refactored into a feature module with typed api.ts/types.ts, hooks, utilities, focused components, a tiny page wrapper, route-level lazy loading, fetch-based SSE with Authorization headers, and an explicit MVP non-streaming chat label.

error_message: npm run build failed in admin-ui: src/features/workbench/api.ts(12,34): error TS2339: Property 'env' does not exist on type 'ImportMeta'.
root_cause: The app did not have Vite client env typings, so TypeScript did not know import.meta.env.
fix: Added admin-ui/src/vite-env.d.ts with vite/client reference and a typed non-secret VITE_GATEWAY_API_BASE override.

error_message: Admin UI login stored JWT under meridian_admin_token, but the shared API client read conduit_admin_token, causing authenticated admin API calls to omit Authorization.
root_cause: Token storage key drift between the auth provider and API client.
fix: Added admin-ui/src/auth/tokenStorage.ts and wired useAuth, the IAM API client, and Workbench gateway API to the same read/write/clear helpers with legacy key fallback.

error_message: Gateway used cross-turn ConversationSession memory despite exposing a stateless OpenAI-compatible API, manufacturing pivot/precedence/reset bugs.
root_cause: Resolved entities and last agent results were stored by conversationId in Redis, then reused on later turns. A stale carried entity could override a newly named entity or survive a client reset.
fix: Made gateway a pure per-request function of messages[] + JWT: removed session store reads/writes, deleted ConversationSession/ConversationSessionStore, removed fixed history truncation, enforced latest user mention wins over user-authored messages, re-resolve/re-check coverage every turn, and clarify deterministically when no user-authored resource reference exists.

error_message: Workbench sent the logged-in admin JWT to the gateway, so entitlement decisions reflected admin instead of the selected demo persona.
root_cause: Workbench had no Axiom-minted persona token exchange and reused readAdminToken() for chat and trace requests.
fix: Added platform-admin-gated /auth/impersonate with audited short-lived JWTs, wired Workbench persona selection from usersApi.list(), and send persona Bearer tokens to chat plus trace stream.

error_message: Users page rendered blank because the UI consumed /users as User[] while IAM returns PageResponse<UserResponse>.
root_cause: usersApi.list() returned the raw paginated response, so Users.tsx called array methods on an object and crashed the route without a boundary.
fix: Mapped usersApi.list() to PageResponse.content, added route ErrorBoundary, visible users error state, and corrected create/update payloads to match IAM DTOs.

error_message: Trace rail could show events from other conversations and retained old events after New conversation.
root_cause: Client whitelisted null conversationId events and the gateway trace stream broadcast every event to every subscriber; local event state was append-only across conversation resets.
fix: Added optional server-side conversationId filtering for trace subscribers, made Workbench connect with the current conversationId, dropped non-matching events, and cleared events/request tracking when persona or conversation changes.

error_message: PolicyForge submitted role UUIDs as subject_roles instead of Cerbos role names.
root_cause: The UI treated RoleResponse.id as the policy role identifier, but Cerbos policies and the policy generator expect role names.
fix: Changed policy role selection to store role.name, surfaced policy data load errors, and kept the Axiom/gold visual treatment consistent.

error_message: Sandboxed smoke/Maven/Python test attempts produced localhost socket errors unrelated to product behavior.
root_cause: macOS Codex sandbox denied localhost sockets for curl/Python/Redis-backed tests.
fix: Stopped/reran the invalid smoke attempt and reran smoke, Maven gateway tests, Playwright, and pytest integration with approved unsandboxed execution.

error_message: Admin console (:5182) browser-origin login → 403 'Invalid CORS request'
root_cause: iam Spring Security CORS allowlist only had :5180 and :3080; the Axiom admin console origin http://localhost:5182 was missing, so even the same-origin nginx /api proxy (which forwards the browser Origin header) was CORS-rejected.
fix: Made the allowed-origins list env-configurable via @Value(${iam.cors.allowed-origins:...}) and added http://localhost:5182 to the default. Rebuilt+recreated iam-service. Preflight :5182→:8084 now 200 with ACAO; proxied login 403→200.

error_message: BFF: 'post-stream persistence failed ... Interrupted waiting for lock' when browser closes mid-stream
root_cause: On client-abort the request's virtual thread is interrupted; the Mongo save then fails its lock acquisition and was logged at ERROR with a full stack trace.
fix: Offloaded post-stream persist to the existing backgroundExecutor (fresh, non-interrupted virtual thread) so the assistant message still persists; added isInterruption() classification so a genuine abort logs at DEBUG (restores interrupt flag) instead of ERROR. Skip empty assistant content. mvn package green.

error_message: Stale Playwright specs target legacy LibreChat (#email on :3080) instead of canonical chat (:8099 Axiom OIDC)
root_cause: Specs pointed at retired LibreChat UI/login. Canonical UI is the :8099 SPA whose AuthGate redirects to Axiom OIDC (#username/#password on :8084).
fix: Rewrote helpers.registerOrLogin to the :8099 OIDC flow (persona login), updated sendMessage/newConversation to Conduit composer selectors, rewrote 00-login + 01-branding for :8099, set playwright baseURL=:8099. tsc --noEmit clean.

error_message: docker build iam-service failed: JwtClaimsCustomizerTest constructor cannot be applied to given types
root_cause: `mvn package -DskipTests` still COMPILES test sources; changing JwtClaimsCustomizer's constructor broke the test. Local `mvn compile` passed (main only) so it was missed until the Docker build.
fix: Updated the test to the new constructor (added AuditService + chatClientId args) and added a chat_access emit test. Always run `mvn test-compile` (not just compile) after changing a public constructor/signature.

error_message: Rebuilt admin-console image not picked up; UI served stale bundle (no Client column)
root_cause: `docker compose build admin-console` without `-p orchestrator-demo` tagged the image under project `orchestrator-chat` (dir name). The service has no explicit `image:`, so build+recreate under different projects used different image tags. iam-service was unaffected because it pins `image: conduit/iam-service:latest`.
fix: Always build with the running project: `docker compose -p orchestrator-demo build <svc>` then `up -d --no-deps --force-recreate <svc>`. Also: iam restart regenerates the RS256 signing key on boot, invalidating previously issued tokens.

error_message: DeepEval release gate FAIL: routing F1=0.0%, every /debug/resolve returned HTTP 401
root_cause: Phase-11 JWT hardening removed the X-User-Id trusted hop (identity now only from verified JWT sub) and made /debug/** require platform_admin/domain_admin. The harness sent X-User-Id and no bearer token, so all resolves 401'd.
fix: Mint gateway-valid RS256 tokens via Axiom /auth/login (admin for /debug/resolve, per-user for /v1/chat). Gate then PASSES at 94.1%. eval-routing.py/worker.py already mint tokens; eval_deepeval.py had been missed.

error_message: Users screen role assignment silently failed / default chat_user role not applied on create (POST /users/{id}/roles -> 400)
root_cause: client.ts usersApi.assignRole sent {role_id} but AssignRoleRequest DTO expects {roleId}; iam has NO global snake_case Jackson strategy (responses are camelCase e.g. isActive/adminDomains), so role_id never bound and validation returned 400.
fix: Changed the POST body to { roleId }. Verified 200 + role now present in UserResponse and in the minted token's roles claim.

error_message: GET /users/{id} returned segments: [] for personas after ABAC V5 migration (edit mode could not repopulate rows)
root_cause: toUserResponse read the segments attribute via getStringList (List<String>), but V5 stored segments as a per-segment MAP {segment->tier}; a JSON object is not a List so it yielded empty.
fix: UserResponse.segments -> Map<String,String>; added getSegmentMap() (map preferred, legacy list tolerated by mapping each segment to the flat classification tier). Update path now merges attributes so identity metadata (display_name/title/department) is not wiped.

error_message: Chat BFF rolling summary never persists (Conversation.summary always null in Mongo); context compaction never attaches a summary (summaryAttached=false every turn), so early turns that fall out of the recent window are forgotten and callback-memory fails.
root_cause: Lost update. ChatStreamService.savePersistently() runs on a background thread after streaming and calls ConversationService.touchAndMaybeTitle(conversation,...), which did a full-document conversationRepository.save(conversation). That 'conversation' was loaded at the START of the turn (before the fire-and-forget LlmSummaryService wrote the summary), so its in-memory summary was stale (null). The full-document save overwrote the summary the summarizer had just persisted — every turn — silently defeating compaction.
fix: Made touchAndMaybeTitle do a field-scoped MongoTemplate.updateFirst($set title+updatedAt) scoped by _id+userId, never writing the summary field. The title/updatedAt writer and the summary writer can no longer race on the summary field. Also added a DEBUG [context] observability log in ContextAssembler showing the assembled summary+recent-window per turn.

error_message: Chat keyword-less follow-up misrouted and wrongly denied: 'can you then summarize for Calderon Trust (REL-00099)?' selected only meridian.insurance.claim_status @0.312, pruned the wealth agents 'below-confidence-floor', then the segment gate denied with 'You do not have access to any of the required services.' Calderon (REL-00099) IS in rm_jane's book.
root_cause: The chat path routed on the BARE last user message (extractLatestUserMessage) with NO domain scope (resolver.resolve(latestPrompt)). A keyword-less follow-up embeds into the cross-domain noise band (~0.30); the porous absolute floor 0.30 let a single out-of-domain agent (insurance @0.312) through while the true wealth agents fell below the floor. The empty-selection CLARIFY fallback was also starved: a lone noise hit kept selected non-empty, so it bypassed clarification and went to a wrong deny. Model-independent (gpt-4o-mini and gpt-5.4-mini identical).
fix: Context-aware routing. (1) Route on conversation-enriched text (recent user turns, bounded by conduit.chat.routing-context-turns) so a keyword-less follow-up inherits the conversation's established domain vocabulary and the true matches rise above the floor. (2) New AgentResolver.resolveContextual(): confidence/abstain gate — route only if the leading candidate clears the floor AND is either decisively strong (>= conduit.resolver.decisive-score) or beats the best OTHER-domain candidate by conduit.resolver.domain-margin; otherwise abstain (empty+fallback) -> deterministic CLARIFY, fixing the starved fallback. Margin-based, not a raw cutoff. Applied the normal dynamic floor to the broad pass (NOT a rigid single-domain re-scope) so legitimate cross-domain fan-out (holdings + settlements) is preserved. (3) CLARIFY-intent turns route on the bare message (carryContext=false) so a terse ask cannot inherit a confident domain and be answered. Domain decision comes from the winning agent manifest's own domain() field — no domain literal in gateway (world-b-check CRITICAL 0). Verified via BFF multi-turn: turn3 Calderon now routes wealth-led (holdings @0.587, no insurance) and answers; turn5 bare 'Calderon Trust?' clean CLARIFY; Okafor out-of-coverage clean deny not insurance; hero cross-domain query still fans out; golden routing eval 91.5%.

error_message: Multi-turn chat: keyword-less/name-only follow-ups wrongly clarified or answered 'no info' even though the entity was named. Turn6 'Now pull up Calderon Trust's holdings' → clarified 'which client, Whitman or Calderon?'; turn2 'performance on that portfolio?' → FOLLOW_UP answered from memory 'no info' (performance never fetched); turn21 'and for REL-00099?' → resolved 0 agents (routing abstained + id dropped). Entity/intent layer failure on multi-turn (routing fix bug-232 was working).
root_cause: Three multi-turn gaps in the entity/intent layer. (1) FOCAL: the combined intent+entity LLM (GLM) reads the whole window and either mis-classified a freshly-named entity as CLARIFY (false Whitman-vs-Calderon ambiguity) or emitted a RECALLED id (e.g. REL-00099 seen in a prior assistant answer) for a named entity; the World-B anti-carryover guard correctly dropped the recalled id but left a null reference → coverage discover→clarify. (2) FACET: a terse id-only follow-up ('and for REL-00099?') abstained at routing (top-vs-noise margin 0.007 < 0.05) AND the LLM dropped the literal id, so 0 agents. (3) BIAS-TO-FETCH: a FOLLOW_UP asking for a not-yet-shown facet ('performance') answered 'no info' from history instead of fetching.
fix: Stateless conversation-focus, re-derived per turn from the client window (no server memory). (1) Focal-entity: IntentClassifier prompt made focal (explicit name in latest message supersedes history; pronoun→last focal name; emit the NAME the user typed, never a recalled id; named entity → FETCH not CLARIFY) + deterministic deriveFocalReference() — (a) id_pattern match in the LATEST user message wins (recovers a dropped literal id, fixes turn21 binding), (b) keep the LLM ref only if verbatim in a USER message (World-B invariant 6), (c) focalIdByNameMatch(): match the latest message's proper-noun tokens to the '<Name> (<id>)' associations the system rendered earlier in the transcript (recovers the focal id for a named entity when GLM emits a recalled id — fixes turn8), (d) lastFocalSingleId(): carry the last single-entity id across a bare back-reference (fixes anaphora turns). (2) Facet-carry / bias-to-fetch at the router: AgentResolver.resolveContextual(text, entityKnown) — when the turn carries an explicit grounded resolvable reference, do NOT abstain on a low margin (coverage CHECK stays the gate); ChatService computes entityKnown from the focal bag + id_pattern (fixes turn21 routing). (3) Bias-to-fetch: IntentClassifier extracts entities for FOLLOW_UP too; ChatService.handleFollowUp falls through to handleFetchData when a single grounded entity + confident route exist (fixes turn2); and a routing-abstained FETCH turn degrades to grounded history synthesis when prior assistant data exists (keeps aggregate turns 15/16 answering). Pinned intent-classifier temperature=0 for turn-to-turn determinism. All prompts compiled from the manifest (entity_types/id_pattern/domain_context); anaphora tokens are language-generic @Value config. world-b-check CRITICAL 0 before AND after. Verified on the 26-turn rm_jane eval: BEFORE 6 broken [2,6,7,8,11,21] → AFTER all answer; no regression on the 13 clean turns; out-of-coverage 9/10/17/24 still refuse.

error_message: Multi-turn anaphor bound to an OLDER focal entity instead of the most-recently-named one. rm_jane: turn 5 'settlement status for Whitman' (focal → REL-00042), turn 6 'and their goals?' returned Calderon's goals (REL-00099) — confidently-wrong data.
root_cause: In deriveFocalReference() the path that accepts the LLM's extracted reference when it appears in ANY user message (userMessagesContain) ran BEFORE the anaphoric recency carry. On a bare-anaphor turn the LLM can latch onto an older entity it saw in history (Calderon); that stale grounded name was returned, so the pronoun binding was at the mercy of the LLM's non-deterministic pick rather than conversation-structure recency.
fix: Reordered precedence and gated it generically: (1) explicit id in latest message; (2) a reference the user NAMES in this turn (LLM value shares a >=4-char word with the latest message → new naming supersedes older focus); (3) RECENCY carry for an anaphoric turn that names no new entity (focalIdByNameMatch then lastFocalSingleId = most-recently-named focal entity) placed BEFORE (4) the grounded-LLM-value fallback. Added sharesWord() helper. No domain literal — operates on manifest id_pattern + language-generic anaphora tokens + transcript structure. Behaves identically cross-domain (proven on insurance uw_sam: anaphor bound to just-named POL-77002, not older POL-77001, via coverage-gate trace). world-b-check CRITICAL 0 before/after.

error_message: Out-of-coverage NAMED entity ('show me the Okafor account holdings') clarified with the RM's OWN clients instead of denying.
root_cause: Under heavy conversational load the intent/entity LLM dropped the freshly-named entity (returned null relationship_reference), so the coverage 'no reference' else-branch dumped the RM's book as a clarification rather than resolving the name.
fix: Added a deterministic, principal-agnostic named-entity backstop (resolveNamedReferenceBackstop + properNounPhrases) in the coverage else-branch: proper-noun phrases from the latest message are resolved via the routed sub-domain's coverage service; a UNIQUE resolution runs the coverage CHECK (honest deny if out of book), only genuinely unresolvable/ambiguous names still clarify.

error_message: Out-of-coverage/unknown wealth ID ('REL-00188?') returned insurance copy 'Which policy are you asking about?'.
root_cause: A bare identifier carries no domain vocabulary, so embedding routing scored it at noise level (0.338) and picked an unrelated domain's agent — surfacing the WRONG domain's clarify/deny copy.
fix: Added DomainManifestStore.identifyByIdPattern (manifest id_pattern -> owning resource-scoped sub-domain + coverage) and a deterministic identifier pre-check at the top of handleFetchData: an id that RESOLVES and is NOT entitled denies with THAT domain's own copy before routing; not-found/ambiguous/allowed fall through unchanged.

error_message: Mixed in/out-of-segment ask silently dropped the denied domain; aggregate ask ('total value across my book') fabricated a summed total.
root_cause: (a) Structural-gate-pruned agents never reached the synthesizer, so a mixed wealth+insurance ask fulfilled wealth and dropped insurance without a note. (b) synthesizeFromHistory's prompt forbade inventing numbers but not COMPUTING/aggregating them, so the model summed two portfolios itself.
fix: (a) Thread structural-gate withheldDomains (from pruned manifests' domain(), World-B clean) into AnswerSynthesizer as WITHHELD sections + prompt rule to state them honestly. (b) Hardened both synthesis prompts to forbid cross-entity aggregation/roll-ups and to say a consolidated roll-up view is needed when an aggregate isn't already present.

error_message: ClarificationComposer failed (RuntimeException) — LLM returned HTTP 401; composed clarify silently fell back to the deterministic template for rm_jane/uw_sam.
root_cause: The composer's API-key env used a TRIPLE-nested compose default ${A:-${B:-${C:-}}}. Docker Compose does not reliably resolve triple-nested ${} defaults, yielding an empty/garbled key → Z.AI 401. Intent classifier worked because it uses only a SINGLE-nested default.
fix: Simplified CONDUIT_LLM_CLARIFICATION_COMPOSER_API_KEY to the single-nest pattern the other services use: ${CONDUIT_LLM_CLARIFICATION_COMPOSER_API_KEY:-${ZAI_API_KEY:-}}. Composed clarify then phrased correctly; the 401 path had ALSO proven the fallback (template served, no crash).

error_message: Clarify printed a NUMBERED list and said 'Reply with the number or relationship ID', but replying with a bare '2' re-clarified (never resolved). Only the name ('Calderon Trust') or the ID ('REL-00099') resolve. The clarify promised a positional-number input the resolver never processes.
root_cause: buildDeterministicClarification enumerated options with '1.'/'2.' and hardcoded 'Reply with the number or relationship ID', while the resolve path only honours name or manifest id_pattern (no positional-index parsing). The composed clarify prompt could also reintroduce numbering. Copy over-promised a capability that does not exist.
fix: Option A (align copy to capability). Deterministic template now lists candidates by NAME (+ identifier) with '- ' bullets (no positional numbers) and invites 'Reply with the <entityNoun> name or identifier', entityNoun from the manifest entity display (World-B: no hardcoded 'relationship ID'). ClarificationComposer system prompt forbids positional numbers / 'reply with the number' and its OPTIONS data list uses bullets not numbers. No positional-index parsing added; resolve/validation/fallback unchanged. Verified live on wealth (rm_jane) + insurance (uw_sam), template + composed; name and ID both still resolve; world-b CRITICAL 0.

error_message: Conversation-Trace Tempo panel could not filter by conversation: it keyed on span.entity.relationship_id (a client-scoped, domain-specific attribute) via an entity_id textbox; pasting a conversation id returned nothing.
root_cause: The gateway root span carried session.id but no conversation.id attribute, and the panel filtered on a relationship-id span attribute — so Tempo search could not be keyed on the conversation.
fix: Set span.setAttribute("conversation.id", conversationId) on the chat.handle root span (World-B clean — opaque id, no domain data); removed the entity_id template var; re-keyed the Tempo panel to { span.conversation.id = "$convId" }. Verified Tempo indexes the attribute and returns the value.

error_message: Langfuse dashboard widgets on the OBSERVATIONS view rendered 'Query needs attention / Internal Server Error'; server log: ClickHouse 'Unknown table expression identifier events_core' on dashboard.executeQuery.
root_cause: The unstable widget-create API defaults minVersion=2. In this Langfuse v3 deployment, min_version=2 makes dashboard.executeQuery compile the OBSERVATIONS view against the v2/v4 'events_core'/'events_observations' ClickHouse tables, which do not exist here (v2 metrics is a v4 write-mode feature). Only the v1 view (table 'observations') is backed by real tables.
fix: Wrote the widget rows directly to Postgres with min_version=1 for all six widgets so dashboard.executeQuery uses the v1 view declarations (observations / scores_numeric / traces) that this v3 deployment actually has. Verified every widget renders populated via a headless Playwright screenshot.

error_message: Continuous eval-worker (conduit-eval-continuous) logged 'No traces found' and produced 0 scores for chat-turn traces; Langfuse trace.output was empty (len 0) for every chat-turn trace though trace.input was populated.
root_cause: AnswerSynthesizer set langfuse.trace.output via Span.current().setAttribute(...) inside synthesize()/synthesizeFromHistory(), assuming Span.current() was the root chat.handle span. By synthesis time the active OTel context is not guaranteed to be the root (the agent fan-out can detach it / llm.synthesize orphaned), so the trace-level output never landed on the root span. ChatService sets langfuse.trace.input directly on the rootSpan reference (works), but output was left to Span.current() (didn't). The eval filter requires a non-empty trace output, so all chat-turn traces were skipped → 0 scores.
fix: Made synthesize()/synthesizeFromHistory() (all overloads) RETURN the accumulated answer String; removed the unreliable Span.current().setAttribute('langfuse.trace.output',…). ChatService now mirrors the returned answer onto the ROOT chat.handle span via a new setTraceOutput(rootSpan, answer) helper (blank skipped) at every answer path (FETCH synthesize, FETCH→history degrade, follow-up, chitchat); threaded rootSpan into handleChitchat. Verified: fresh rm_jane chat-turn trace now has trace.output len 474; eval iteration scored it grounding/relevance/safety/partial_honesty=1.00; ClickHouse scores count went 0→4. world-b CRITICAL 0 before/after.

error_message: Three Conduit Insights panels showed hardcoded 'not available yet' EmptyState (Grounding distribution, Grounding by model, Memory compaction) despite the backing data existing.
root_cause: The panels were never added to BoardCatalog board 7 and the UI had hardcoded EmptyState blocks instead of chart components bound to panel data. Grounding-by-model also needed a score->model join because Langfuse writes the grounding score at trace level (observationId null), so the model must be looked up from the trace's GENERATION observation.
fix: Added grounding_distribution (scores->10-bin histogram), grounding_by_model (LangfuseMetricsSource.groundingByModel joins score.traceId to model via modelByTrace over GENERATION observations), and compaction (Prometheus chat_compaction_* counters, no gateway->BFF call) to board 7. UI: replaced the 3 EmptyStates with ScoreHistogram, Bars(unit=score) and CompactionStat, graceful fallback on unavailable. Grounding score name is config (conduit.insights.grounding-score-name). Gotcha: compaction tokensSaved = full-context tokens = 0 and attached% = 0 in current data because no summary has been attached yet (attached=false for all 18) — that is real telemetry, not a bug; panel renders 0 honestly.

error_message: Insights Overview 'P95 LATENCY' KPI reads 0ms; Grafana Business Overview 'Agent Status' table p95 ms and Error % columns show NaN; Grafana Agent Health 'Protocol Latency Comparison — HTTP vs MCP' panel shows 'No numeric fields found' — despite latency data existing.
root_cause: 1) Insights fanout_avg_ms panel (feeds the 'p95 latency' KPI) divided by clamp_min(sum(rate(count)),1); with a sub-1/s call rate the denominator was pinned to the floor of 1, collapsing ~4.5s to ~1.6ms (~0ms). The working latency_by_agent query used floor 0.0001. 2) Grafana Agent Status p95 used histogram_quantile(0.95, rate(bucket[5m])) and Error% used rate(...[5m])/rate(...[5m]); the 5m window had no traffic (demo traffic older than 5m) -> empty buckets -> histogram_quantile=NaN and 0/0=NaN. Error% also lacked sum-by-agentId aggregation (status-label mismatch on the division). 3) Protocol Latency barchart used a range query + timeSeriesTable transform, which yields a sparkline 'Trend' field, not a scalar numeric the barchart can plot -> 'No numeric fields found' (the 5m window was empty too).
fix: 1) Repointed fanout_avg_ms panel to a genuine p95: histogram_quantile(0.95, sum(rate(conduit_agent_latency_seconds_bucket[w])) by (le)) * 1000 (kept panel id for the web contract, title -> 'P95 latency'). Now returns ~8.7s. 2) Grafana p95 (refId D): histogram_quantile(0.95, sum by (agentId, le) (rate(conduit_agent_latency_seconds_bucket[$__range]))) * 1000. Error% (refId C): 100 * (sum by (agentId)(rate(calls{status!=OK}[$__range])) or (sum by (agentId)(rate(calls[$__range]))*0)) / clamp_min(sum by (agentId)(rate(calls[$__range])),1e-9) — $__range follows the dashboard window, or-zero yields 0.0% instead of blank. 3) Protocol panel: made the query instant+table (range=false) and swapped timeSeriesTable for an organize transform hiding Time, so the barchart gets protocol (category) + numeric Value; renders http 2.40s vs mcp 6.70s.

error_message: AgentManifest.io dropped when manifests re-stamped through AgentIntrospector.withDerived and AgentRegistry.stampAndStore — both used the old-arity (19-arg) constructor that sets io=null, so DagResolver saw MISSING_PRODUCER over the real registry.
root_cause: AgentManifest gained an optional `io` component plus a backward-compatible 19-arg delegating constructor (io=null). The two registry re-stamp sites still called that old-arity ctor, silently discarding io on every snapshot rebuild.
fix: Passed the source manifest's io() through the full canonical constructor at both re-stamp sites (s.io() in withDerived, manifest.io() in stampAndStore). Added DagRealManifestTest asserting io survives the load+re-stamp round-trip and that the old-arity ctor drops io (regression guard).

error_message: RegistryBootstrapLoader: Skipping invalid manifest 'meridian.wealth.concentration.json': property 'io' is not defined in the schema and the schema does not allow additional properties. Running gateway loaded only 13 agents; DAG never fired (concentration absent from registry).
root_cause: ManifestValidator validates against the CLASSPATH schema (/agent-manifest.schema.json from gateway/src/main/resources), which was stale — it lacked the optional 'io' block and had additionalProperties:false at root, so every io-bearing manifest was rejected at bootstrap. registry/agent-manifest.schema.json (the pinned copy) had 'io'; the two had drifted.
fix: Synced the bundled classpath schema from the pinned registry copy (cp registry/agent-manifest.schema.json gateway/src/main/resources/). Now io-bearing manifests validate; concentration registers → 14 agents. mvn test still 90/90.

error_message: meridian.wealth.concentration FAILED: 422 Unprocessable Entity 'holdings payload has no positions to analyse' when the DAG fired holdings→concentration.
root_cause: concentration.io declared TWO from-consumes (wealth.holdings required + wealth.risk_profile optional). The resolver pulled in BOTH producers (holdings + risk_profile); both ran and produced, so Blackboard.bind took its 2-producer MERGE branch and wrapped each output under its produces[].name → {holdings:{...}, risk_profile:{...}}. The concentration wire endpoint (HoldingsPayload) expects the holdings fields at TOP LEVEL, so top-level 'positions' was absent → 422. The Blackboard merge convention (wrap-by-name) does not match the concentration agent's flat pass-through wire contract.
fix: Removed the optional {from: wealth.risk_profile} edge from concentration's io so the fan-in is a clean single-producer pass-through (holdings output piped straight through). concentration now returns 200 with real single-name %/HHI/breach flags. risk_profile was 'context annotation, never required' per the agent, so no functional loss. Manifest-only change (World-B clean, no gateway Java). Follow-up seam: align Blackboard multi-producer merge with agent wire contracts before re-adding optional producers.

error_message: F-IDENTITY: caller JWT never reached downstream agents. HttpAdapter/McpAdapter read SecurityContextHolder.getContext().getAuthentication() to get the bearer token, but the request pipeline runs on a virtual-thread executor (ChatCompletionsController's pipelineExecutor) and the DAG executor runs on its own virtual threads — SecurityContextHolder is thread-local and is NOT propagated there, so the token was always null and no Authorization header was ever sent to agents.
root_cause: Thread-local SecurityContextHolder read on the wrong thread — no DelegatingSecurityContextExecutor / inheritable strategy anywhere in the request pipeline, and the offload to virtual threads happens before any agent call.
fix: Capture the caller's bearer token on the SERVLET thread in ChatCompletionsController (extractBearerToken(), reading SecurityContextHolder before CompletableFuture.runAsync) and thread it explicitly as a `callerToken` parameter: ChatCompletionsController -> ChatService.handleChat -> handleFetchData/handleFollowUp/tryDag -> FlatPlanExecutor.execute(plan, callerToken) / DagPlanExecutor.execute(plan, blackboard, callerToken) -> AgentHarness.execute(node, exec, callerToken) -> ProtocolAdapter.invoke(manifest, input, bearerToken). HttpAdapter/McpAdapter now use the PASSED token and throw IllegalStateException (surfaced as a FAILED NodeResult, never a silent no-auth call) if it is null/blank on a data-plane call.

error_message: mock-agents jwt_verify.py failed OPEN on a missing/invalid token: 'if not authorization: return True' (allow), plus a second bypass 'if not token or token == "unused": return True' — meaning ANY caller could reach a data endpoint with zero auth, or trivially bypass verification entirely by sending 'Authorization: Bearer unused'.
root_cause: Deliberate 'gateway is the trust boundary' design assumption that was never actually true once F-IDENTITY (bug-248) is accounted for — hop-level auth was effectively off and untestable. The 'Bearer unused' bypass was a stale accommodation with no live caller (grepped: not referenced in any librechat.yaml/.env/compose config).
fix: Both branches now fail CLOSED: missing header -> 401 'Missing Authorization header'; empty bearer value -> 401 'Empty bearer token' (the 'unused' special-case was removed entirely — it now falls through to the normal invalid-format rejection). Existing per-service middleware exemptions (/health, /openapi.json, /docs, /redoc) are unchanged. servicing-mcp's JwtAuthMiddleware additionally gained a narrow exemption for the MCP introspection handshake (JSON-RPC 'initialize'/'tools/list' with no Authorization) since the gateway's boot-time McpToolIntrospector legitimately has no caller identity; 'tools/call' (the real data-plane invocation) always requires a valid token.

error_message: wealth-http log: "Failed to fetch JWKS from http://iam-service:8084/.well-known/jwks.json: Redirect response '301'" followed by "JWKS unreachable — allowing kid=... (dev fallback)" on every single agent call — real RS256 signature verification was NEVER actually happening at the agent hop; every call silently rode the JWKS-unreachable dev-fallback allow path.
root_cause: jwt_verify.py's JWKS_URL default pointed at http://iam-service:8084/.well-known/jwks.json, which 301-redirects to /oauth2/jwks; httpx.get() does not follow redirects by default, so the fetch always raised and hit the (separately legitimate) 'JWKS endpoint unreachable -> dev fallback allow' branch. The gateway's OWN JWKS client (CONDUIT_AUTH_JWKS_URL) was already correctly pointed at /oauth2/jwks directly, so this asymmetry had been masking the agent-side verification gap.
fix: Changed JWKS_URL default to http://iam-service:8084/oauth2/jwks (matching the gateway's working config) — no redirect hop, so JWKS fetch succeeds and real signature verification executes. Verified post-fix: docker exec into wealth-http running verify_bearer_token() with a real rm_jane JWT returns allowed=True with full decoded claims (sub=rm_jane, permissions, segments) instead of a fallback allow with claims=None.

error_message: integration-test.sh: 'Stream missing [DONE] terminator' and 'Title request did not return a proper SSE stream'
root_cause: Assertion grepped for 'data:[DONE]' (no space), but the gateway correctly emits the OpenAI wire shape 'data: [DONE]' (AnswerSynthesizer.java:264 sends the payload as " [DONE]" with a leading space). Pre-existing test bug, NOT a regression — proven byte-identical on baseline Boot 3.5.0/JDK21.
fix: Corrected both greps to assert the exact OpenAI shape 'data: [DONE]'. Strengthens the byte-contract assertion rather than loosening it.

error_message: e2e-matrix.sh case #04 rm_carlos: expect=allow but deny=yes -> FAIL, despite a correct grounded answer
root_cause: Two bugs. (1) The deny detector was a loose prose grep ('outside', 'not.*access'), so a CORRECT partial answer (hard rule d: harvest survivors, state what's missing — 'asset-servicing data was not included because it is outside your access') was misclassified as a denial. (2) expect=deny cases passed on a denial PHRASE alone with grounding regex rx='x' (a placeholder), so a response that LEAKED data while uttering a denial phrase would have falsely PASSED. Pre-existing, reproduced identically on baseline JDK21.
fix: A full denial is now defined as: refusal phrase AND no domain data returned. Replaced rx='x' on all 5 deny cases with real leak regexes (names/figures that must NOT appear). Net: deny cases now also assert zero data leakage — strictly stronger than before.
